story.text: As bdo, I am sometimes here but AFK, and I want a session to just keep working — stopping if and when it actually needs me, and going silent the moment it escalates — so I do not have to prompt and prompt to keep it moving. Every ambient beat this repo has (summon, mock-shame, owner-ask-shame, reflect-auto) fires on a NEW prompt or surfaces drift; none of them makes a session CONTINUE on its own when a turn ends. This is that beat: a Stop-hook patrol that, when a turn ends, nudges the session to carry the next gap unless it has escalated, hit a safety ceiling, or the only work left is the owner's stamp. It is the session-level instance of the landing-throughput principle 'the patrol stays silent, no bureaucracy' — it senses and nudges, it never judges, and it adds no approval layer.

briefing.headline: the continue-beat: ambient auto-continuation that goes silent on escalation
briefing.value: loop/patrol.py (a pure decide() fold + a write-once escalate verb) and .claude/hooks/continue_beat.py (a Stop hook). When a session's turn ends, the beat composes loop.gaps.top_gap and loop.summon.owner_backlog (it imports them, never re-derives — I-4) and decides: CONTINUE (block the stop, feed back the next gap) when there is self-serviceable work and no escalation; SILENT (let the session stop) when the session armed the escalate marker, the per-session beat ceiling is reached, or the only work left is bdo's stamp. The escalate marker is gitignored nag-state (.ai-native/continue-beat.json, the mock-shame.json precedent), never the truth log.
briefing.why_now: bdo asked for it directly (2026-06-18): 'an ambient trigger which reminds agents to continue working… stopping if and when they need me, and if the request is escalate then they stop the reminders. Otherwise I don't need to prompt and prompt.' Confirmed the shape via the /ask panel: in-session beat now, silence on needs-you / parked-at-stamp / escalate plus a ceiling, and an empty backlog does NOT silence (the idle default is to find the next work).
briefing.if_accepted: A live session keeps working the backlog with no prompting from bdo, and stands itself down the instant it genuinely needs him. The honest limit is named: a Stop hook continues a RUNNING session; it cannot wake a process that already exited — true AFK 'wake it later' is a separate timed layer (a scheduled cloud routine), deferred to its own done-line.
briefing.if_rejected: Keeping a session moving stays a manual chore — bdo prompts 'continue' again and again while AFK, and a session that finishes a turn just stops even when the harness-generated backlog is full.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and fail-SAFE-to-STOP: the hook continues only on an explicit derived decision and lets the session stop on ANY internal error or malformed payload (the opposite of the other beats' fail-open) — so a bug can never trap an AFK session burning tokens. The CEILING is a hard per-session backstop. It writes only gitignored nag-state. Reverting is removing one Stop-hook line and deleting two files.
briefing.mechanism: loop/patrol.py: decide(state, session_id, gap, owner_present) is pure (no IO) so the teeth can hold it to derived behaviour; decide_from_root gathers the live folds and persists; arm_escalate writes the marker. .claude/hooks/continue_beat.py prints {"decision":"block","reason":<the next move>} to continue, or nothing to stop, and fail-safe-to-stop on any error. tests/test_patrol.py joins the suite with the §10 teeth: a constant decider FAILS (has-work must continue AND escalated must go silent — both outcomes proven reachable; the ceiling, parked-at-stamp, and new-session-reset each pinned; the hook's fail-safe proven by subprocess on malformed stdin and an unimportable root). Done-line 0127; full suite green at 918.
